pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py lines 6, 98, 114; export_result.sh lines 20, 41; Web search confirmed multiparameter monitors are real medical devices used in clinical bedside monitoring
Code analysis: verifier.py lines 7, 98, 114; export_result.sh lines 25, 46; Web search confirmed pulse oximeters are real medical devices used in patient transport and monitoring
Code analysis: verifier.py lines 8, 99, 114; export_result.sh lines 30, 51; Web search confirmed OpenICE has a documented Vital Signs application (vitals.html) that displays vital sign data
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 56 defines OVERLAP_SCREENSHOT_PATH, setup_task.sh line 31 cleans it before task start
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 66 defines LOG_REPORT_PATH, setup_task.sh line 32 cleans it before task start

Code analysis: verifier.py line 173 checks `passed = score >= 60`. Scoring breakdown: Bedside Monitor (10pts), Transport Monitor (10pts), Vital Signs (10pts), Overlap Screenshot (20pts), Disconnection (15pts), Continuity (10pts), Parallel Sequence (20pts), Documentation (5pts) = 100pts max.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetic medical device data from the OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment) simulator platform. The bedside device is a Multiparameter Monitor, the transport device is a Pulse Oximeter, and the clinical application is the Vital Signs monitoring app. All devices are virtual simulations within OpenICE, not real physical devices or real patient data. The task requires capturing an overlap state screenshot at /home/ga/Desktop/handoff_overlap.png and creating a documentation log at /home/ga/Desktop/transport_log.txt. The pass threshold is 60 points out of a maximum of 100 points.
